---
title: "Jackknife Sampling"
---

The jackknife method provides unbiased estimates of the mean and standard
error by systematically leaving out one block of configurations at a time.
Unlike bootstrap, it produces an exact (unbiased) estimate of the mean by
construction.

**Source file**: `jackknife.py`

---

## The `JackknifeSamples` class

`JackknifeSamples` is a subclass of `AbstractSamples` (and therefore of
`np.ndarray`). Each row $i$ stores the mean computed from all
configurations *except* block $i$.

```python
from lattice_data_tools.jackknife import JackknifeSamples
```

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`
**ID**: `JKF-JackknifeSamples`
:::

As with `BootstrapSamples`, direct use of `np.mean`, `np.std`, and
`np.average` is blocked.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`, method `__array_function__`
**ID**: `JKF-JackknifeSamples-__array_function__`
:::

---

## Statistical estimators

### `.mean()`

```python
jkf.mean() -> np.ndarray
```

Jackknife mean — the arithmetic average of all jackknife samples along
axis 0.  This is unbiased by construction.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`
**ID**: `JKF-JackknifeSamples-mean`
:::

### `.error()`

```python
jkf.error() -> np.ndarray
```

Jackknife standard error:

$$\delta\bar{x} = \sqrt{N_\text{jkf} - 1} \cdot \sigma_\text{jkf}$$

where $\sigma_\text{jkf}$ is the standard deviation of the jackknife
samples and $N_\text{jkf}$ is the number of samples.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`
**ID**: `JKF-JackknifeSamples-error`
:::

---

## Generating jackknife samples

### `uncorrelated_confs_to_jkf`

```python
uncorrelated_confs_to_jkf(x, N_jkf) -> JackknifeSamples
```

Generate $N_\text{jkf}$ jackknife samples from uncorrelated data `x`.
Configurations are divided into blocks of size $\lfloor N/N_\text{jkf} \rfloor$;
each sample omits one block.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`
**ID**: `JKF-uncorrelated_confs_to_jkf`
:::

```python
import numpy as np
from lattice_data_tools.jackknife import uncorrelated_confs_to_jkf

data = np.random.normal(5.0, 1.0, 200)
jkf  = uncorrelated_confs_to_jkf(data, N_jkf=20)
print(jkf.mean(), "±", jkf.error())
```

### `correlated_confs_to_jkf`

```python
correlated_confs_to_jkf(Cg, N_jkf, output_file=None) -> JackknifeSamples
```

Same as above, but for correlated Monte Carlo chains.  The integrated
autocorrelation time $\tau_\text{int}$ is estimated via `uwerr_primary`
and configurations are thinned accordingly before jackknifing.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source**: `jackknife.py`
**ID**: `JKF-correlated_confs_to_jkf`
:::

---

## Bootstrap vs. Jackknife

| Property | Bootstrap | Jackknife |
|---|---|---|
| Mean estimator | biased (`.mean()`) + unbiased (`.unbiased_mean()`) | unbiased (`.mean()`) |
| Error estimator | std dev of replicates | $\sqrt{N-1}\,\sigma_\text{jkf}$ |
| Autocorrelation handling | auto-binning via $\tau_\text{int}$ | thinning via $\tau_\text{int}$ |
| Non-linear propagation | yes | yes |
| Computational cost | $O(N_\text{bts})$ | $O(N_\text{jkf})$ |

Both classes share the `AbstractSamples` interface, so code that calls
`.mean()` and `.error()` works identically for both.
